title: Version ranges trade reproducibility for silent updates
description: Open version ranges let transitive dependencies change without a code change, so a pinned lockfile is what actually makes installs reproducible and reviewable.
body: Use when reasoning about why a build changed with no code edit. A caret/tilde range in a manifest permits new matching versions to resolve at install time, and transitive dependencies float even more, so two installs days apart can pull different code; the lockfile pins the exact resolved tree that was reviewed. Do not treat the manifest's ranges as the installed versions. Actions: commit and install from the lockfile, review lockfile diffs as real changes, and update versions deliberately. Validation signals include a lockfile diff in code review and identical installs from the same lockfile. Risk boundary: an unreviewed lockfile change can introduce a malicious transitive version; treat lockfile diffs as security-relevant and escalate unexpected ones rather than auto-merging.
